item.title: Japan lifts decades-old ban on lethal arms exports in major policy shift
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, marking a significant shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

item.title: China's youth unemployment rises to 16.9% ahead of record graduation season
item.description: China's official youth jobless rate climbed to 16.9% in March, with another 12.7 million graduates set to enter the workforce, deepening the employment crunch.

item.title: China bets on coal conversion to secure energy amid Iran war disruption
item.description: Far from its eastern metropolises, China is doubling down on coal as a core element of its energy and industrial security strategy, seeking to reduce vulnerability to supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Three ships including Iranian vessel attempt Hormuz transits
item.description: Three vessels, including one Iranian-flagged ship and two others with unclear ownership, attempted to transit the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing tensions and blockade concerns.

item.title: Major Indonesian natural gas discovery announced
item.description: A significant natural gas find in Indonesia could unlock substantial new volumes for both domestic consumption and international export markets.

item.title: US delays weapons deliveries to European allies as Iran war strains stockpiles
item.description: The Pentagon has informed European countries including Baltic and Scandinavian states that previously contracted weapons deliveries will be delayed as the Iran conflict draws on US munitions stocks. The delays highlight how the war that began in February is stretching American military supplies.

item.title: Rubio defends Maduro capture to wary Caribbean leaders
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told Caribbean leaders that Venezuela is better off today after the Trump administration military operation to capture Nicolás Maduro. Caribbean Community bloc members raised concerns about the legality of the raid while Rubio promoted the Donroe Doctrine for US dominance in the Western Hemisphere.

item.title: Oil prices jump 4% as Trump announces seizure of Iranian cargo ship
item.description: Brent crude rose above $94 per barrel after President Trump said the US Navy intercepted an Iran-flagged vessel. Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz again after a temporary reopening, threatening to target any ship that approaches the waterway critical to global oil supplies.

item.title: British universities paid firm to spy on pro-Palestine students
item.description: An Al Jazeera investigation reveals 12 elite UK universities paid Horus Security Consultancy, run by former military intelligence officials, to monitor student protesters including pro-Palestinian activists. The firm harvested social media data and conducted counter-terror assessments on academics.

item.title: Kuwait declares force majeure on oil shipments as Hormuz blockade blocks exports
item.description: Kuwait declared force majeure on crude and refined product shipments after the Strait of Hormuz blockade prevented vessels from entering the Gulf, hampering its ability to meet customer commitments.

item.title: South Korea president rejects claim minister leaked US intel on North Korea
item.description: President Lee Jae Myung dismissed as "absurd" allegations that his minister overseeing relations with Pyongyang revealed classified US intelligence on North Korean nuclear facilities.

item.title: Russia values seized gold producer stake at $1.9 billion ahead of auction
item.description: The Russian property agency valued a 67.2% stake in gold producer UGC at 140.4 billion roubles ahead of a May auction to sell the seized assets.

item.title: UK stocks fall as Iran ceasefire uncertainty lifts oil prices
item.description: Britain's main stock indexes dropped in broad-based losses as fears the US-Iran ceasefire could collapse weighed on investor sentiment.

item.title: Erdogan vows tighter gun laws after deadly Turkish school shooting
item.description: Turkey will tighten gun-ownership rules and increase penalties after rare deadly school shootings sparked national outrage.

item.title: Israel Establishes "Yellow Line" in South Lebanon Despite Ceasefire
item.description: Israel has created a "Yellow Line" buffer zone in southern Lebanon near the border, where its forces continue to operate despite a 10-day ceasefire with Hezbollah, raising concerns about long-term territorial control.

item.title: Philippines-US Launch Largest Balikatan Exercises Near Taiwan
item.description: The largest-ever Balikatan military exercises began between Philippine and US forces, including maritime strike drills on an island near Taiwan, as Washington seeks to reassure Asian allies amid the Iran crisis.

item.title: Ukraine Launches 5% Leasing Program for Defense Industry
item.description: Ukraine named three state banks to provide preferential equipment leasing to defense manufacturers at a 5% annual rate, aiming to boost domestic arms production capacity.

item.title: UAE Thwarts Iran-Linked Terror Plot, Arrests Network Members
item.description: UAE authorities dismantled an Iran-linked terror network that planned sabotage operations, recruited Emirati youth, and channeled funds to suspicious foreign entities.

item.title: Turkey Defense R&D to Boost Other Sectors, Official Says
item.description: Turkish defense industry R&D achievements could serve as a catalyst for other sectors, according to officials highlighting the spillover benefits of military technology investments.

item.title: Gulf states fear US-Iran talks may cement Tehran's control over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Gulf Arab states worry that ongoing US-Iran negotiations may only achieve reopening the strategic Strait of Hormuz, falling short of broader de-escalation they deem essential for regional stability.

item.title: Oil prices fall as markets bet on US-Iran peace talks proceeding
item.description: Crude prices dropped on expectations that US-Iran negotiations will move forward this week, potentially allowing more oil supply to flow from the Middle East and easing global energy concerns.

item.title: Israel tightens grip on south Lebanon despite ceasefire
item.description: Israel warned residents to stay out of a belt of territory at the Lebanese border, entrenching its control over southern Lebanon despite the ceasefire in the war with Hezbollah.

item.title: Russia detains German woman in alleged Ukrainian-directed terror plot
item.description: Russia's FSB security service detained a 57-year-old German woman with explosives, claiming it was a false-flag operation orchestrated by Ukrainian intelligence.

item.title: Russia-friendly former president Rumen Radev sweeps Bulgaria election
item.description: Rumen Radev, a pro-Russia former fighter pilot, won a landslide victory in Bulgaria's snap parliamentary election, potentially shifting the EU and NATO member closer to Moscow.

item.title: China warns US-Philippines-Japan drills risk regional trust
item.description: China criticized expanded military cooperation as US and Philippines began annual exercises with Japan participation. The drills include maritime strike operations near Taiwan.
